summary: Recent updates to the Media Capabilities within Firefox have focused on enhancing support for **WebRTC, the technology behind video calls**, ensuring it operates more smoothly and efficiently. This includes creating a mechanism to validate WebRTC’s scalability settings and implementing allowlists for various MediaCapabilities types to streamline support checks. Additional improvements have also been made to how the browser handles audio and video codecs, enhancing its ability to decode and encode content while optimizing power efficiency. These changes are critical as they not only improve the performance of real-time communications but also ensure broader compatibility with various media formats and configurations, making for a better user experience during streaming and video calls.
